entry.title: Re: Error with automatic flexible Workflow for purchase r...
entry.content:
Hi,

Please check the failed workflow instance in the Workflows in Status Error app:

  1. Open Workflows in Status Error.
  2. Select the workflow for Overall Release of Purchase Requisition.
  3. Open the affected workflow instance and choose Display Work Item.
  4. Review the Work Item Log for the detailed error message.

If a specific business error is displayed, correct the corresponding data issue in the Purchase Requisition and then use Resume After Error.

If no detailed error is available in the work item log, further analysis is required because the current error (MMPUR_PR_WFL_COMMON008) is a generic callback failure.

If required, a controlled change to the Purchase Requisition can be performed to retrigger workflow processing, but the changed field should be one that is configured to restart Flexible Workflow according to Manage Conditions to Restart Flexible Workflow or the workflow restart logic.

Kindly refer to the KBA  3402028 - Error in 'Workflows in Status Error 'App or Red mark on Approval details tab - SAP for Me for further instructions.

Thanks and regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) for LifeSciences & Healthcare: Building the Data Foundation for AI Era
entry.content:
The Data Challenge in Life Sciences and Healthcare Industry

Life Sciences and Healthcare organizations operate in one of the most data-intensive, highly regulated environments in the global economy. From clinical trial management and pharmacovigilance to patient outcome tracking and drug supply chain compliance, these organizations generate and consume massive volumes of data across disparate systems, geographies, and regulatory jurisdictions.

Yet despite significant investments in data and AI, many organizations struggle to realize transformative value. The reason is not a lack of data. It is a lack of shared business context that connects data to the processes, decisions, and outcomes that matter.

This challenge manifests as what SAP calls the complexity tax: multi-cloud sprawl, disconnected data across systems, and a critical absence of the semantic context that AI requires to deliver meaningful results. For an industry where a single regulatory non-compliance event can cost hundreds of millions, and where time-to-market for therapies directly impacts patient lives, this tax is not merely an IT inefficiency. It is a business risk.

Why Yesterday's Data Architecture Falls Short

Traditional data architectures were designed for a deterministic world. Optimized for applications and dashboards, they prioritized price-performance for structured reporting, predefined models, and static analytical outputs. This worked for generating quarterly reports and operational dashboards.

But Life Sciences and Healthcare now demand far more:

  • Clinical supply chain orchestration requires real-time data integration across manufacturing, logistics, regulatory, and partner systems.
  • Regulatory compliance (such as the U.S. Drug Supply Chain Security Act) requires end-to-end traceability and lineage across the entire product lifecycle.
  • Patient outcome analysis demands the ability to correlate data across electronic health records, clinical data warehouses, commercial operations, and real-world evidence sources.
  • AI-driven pharmacovigilance needs not just access to adverse event data, but deep understanding of how that data relates to manufacturing batches, distribution channels, and patient populations.

AI does not wait for insight; it acts on it. And acting requires continuous, context-rich understanding across systems, domains, and processes. The old architecture simply cannot deliver this.

SAP Business Data Cloud: A Data Fabric Built for the AI Era

SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) represents a fundamental architectural shift. Rather than treating data as something to be moved, stored, and queried, BDC establishes a business data fabric where data is continuously connected to business context and made available for AI-driven decision-making and autonomous action.

The architecture consists of four integrated layers:

1. Lake Storage

BDC unifies SAP and non-SAP data across hybrid and cloud environments, creating a consistent foundation for both structured and unstructured data. For Life Sciences organizations, this means connecting data from SAP S/4HANA, SAP ECC, clinical trial systems, laboratory information management systems, and third-party data providers into a single accessible layer, without requiring costly data replication.

2. Intelligent Compute

Powered by SAP HANA Cloud, SAP Databricks, and SAP Snowflake, the intelligent compute layer provides flexible execution across diverse workloads. SAP HANA Cloud's multi-model processing (including graph, vector, spatial, and time-series capabilities) is particularly relevant for Life Sciences, where understanding relationships between molecules, patients, clinical sites, and regulatory requirements is fundamental to the business.

3. Knowledge Core

The Knowledge Core is where BDC fundamentally differentiates itself. It brings together:

  • Business semantics that define how metrics, KPIs, and data entities are interpreted consistently across the organization
  • Knowledge graphs that map relationships across processes and domains
  • Data products that deliver trusted, business-ready data
  • Analytics and simulations that enable scenario planning and trade-off analysis
  • Active metadata that captures how data is used, governed, and evolving

For Life Sciences and Healthcare, this means that when an AI agent evaluates a supply chain disruption, it does so with full understanding of regulatory requirements, batch genealogy, patient safety implications, and commercial commitments. Context is built into the system, not rebuilt for every use case.

4. AI Agents and Intelligent Applications

At the top of the architecture, SAP Joule orchestrates AI agents that turn context into autonomous action. These agents can execute multi-step, cross-application workflows grounded in business understanding. Purpose-built models trained on business data deliver domain-specific insights, while governance ensures every autonomous action remains traceable and compliant.

Data Products: The Building Blocks 

At the core of BDC's value proposition are data products: semantically-enriched, well-described, discoverable, and easily consumable data sets that serve as the fundamental building blocks for analytics and AI.

For Life Sciences and Healthcare, this means:

  • SAP-managed data products delivered out-of-the-box for common business domains (Finance, Supply Chain, Procurement, HR)
  • Customer-managed data products that extend and enrich the foundation with organization-specific clinical, regulatory, or commercial data including third-party data (market intelligence, real-world evidence, regulatory databases) into the same governed framework

SAP establishes a data product economy that serves multiple use cases through bidirectional, zero-copy Delta Share. Data products can be SAP-managed (delivered directly from SAP LoB source systems) or customer-managed (from customer-managed source systems, partners, or Databricks).

Currently available Data Products are listed on SAP Business Accelerator Hub. Upcoming Data Products and BDC product roadmap can be explored on SAP Road Map Explorer.

AI Agents: From Insight to Autonomous Action

SAP Business Data Cloud closes the loop between data and execution by providing ready-to-run insights with out-of-the-box analytical models, dashboards, and workflows. These are infused with AI/ML to generate recommendations and automate actions, curated and managed by SAP and SAP partners, and automatically connected to your data for accuracy and accelerated time to insights.

Context-aware agents are grounded in authoritative business context and governed metadata, eliminating silos between agents with a shared understanding of the business. SAP delivers intelligent content for each Line of Business (LoB):

  • Cloud ERP Intelligence (CFO) — Working Capital insights, Sustainability Control Tower
  • Finance Intelligence (CFO) — Cash Flow, General Ledger, Billing & Payments
  • People Intelligence (CHRO) — Workforce Composition, Skills, Compensation, Career Development
  • Spend Intelligence (CPO) — Spend Analysis & Insights, Invoices, Contracts, Purchase Orders
  • Supply Chain Intelligence (COO) — Logistics, Manufacturing, Inventory, Deliveries
  • Revenue Intelligence (CRO) — Customer 360, Customer Outreach Timing & Risk Signals

 

The Context Premium

SAP introduces the concept of the context premium: the competitive advantage gained by organizations that preserve and apply business context to their data and AI investments. In Life Sciences and Healthcare, this premium is particularly significant:

  • When AI agents share a unified understanding of the business, procurement decisions account for regulatory constraints, finance decisions account for clinical timelines, and compliance decisions account for commercial impact.
  • When data products carry semantic meaning, new AI use cases can be deployed without months of data preparation and context reconstruction.
  • When governance is embedded by design, regulatory audits become a matter of querying the system rather than assembling evidence from disconnected sources.

Business Capabilities and Getting Started

SAP Business Data Cloud enables organizations to move from fragmented data to a unified, AI-ready environment that drives both operational and strategic impact:

  • Simplify data landscapes across clouds
  • Establish automated governance and trust
  • Deliver business-ready data products with universal business context
  • Deploy context-aware agents and intelligent content
  • Optimize compute intelligently with FinOps stewardship
  • Transform outcomes with Joule Agents

Organizations can explore SAP Business Data Cloud through the product tour, free trial, Demo Hub, or a complimentary modernization assessment for SAP BW, BW/4HANA, BPC, BusinessObjects, and HANA environments.

Conclusion

For Life Sciences and Healthcare organizations, SAP Business Data Cloud is not merely a data platform upgrade. It is a strategic shift from managing data as a cost center to leveraging data as a continuously compounding business asset. By establishing a Knowledge Core that understands the unique complexity of regulated industries, embedding governance at every layer, and enabling AI agents to act with full business context, BDC addresses the fundamental challenge that has limited the return on data and AI investments across the industry.

The organizations that establish this foundation now will be the ones that achieve the context premium: faster drug development cycles, stronger regulatory postures, better patient outcomes, and more efficient commercial operations, all powered by AI that truly understands how their business operates.

entry.title: How to Automatically Remove WBS Element Restrictions Upon Release Using Project Events
entry.content:
Introduction

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, the Enterprise Project Management BO (I_EnterpriseProjectTP_3) publishes standard business
events that customer developers can consume to react to project changes.

This blog walks you through a practical example:

when a WBS element's Processing Status is changed to Released, automatically clear all its Block Function restrictions —

without any custom UI or manual intervention.


Business Scenario

Project managers often set block functions on WBS elements during planning to prevent premature postings (time
recording, purchasing, service posting, etc.).

Once a WBS element is formally released, these restrictions should be lifted automatically.

By consuming the standard EntProjElmntChgd_2 event, you can implement this logic cleanly without
modifying any standard code.


Prerequisites

- SAP S/4HANA Cloud development environment
- Basic understanding of RAP (ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model)
- User assigned with following roles:
    SAP_BR_PROJ_FIN_CONTROLLER
    SAP_BR_DEVELOPER 

- Access to ABAP Development Tools (ADT / Eclipse)


Step 1 : Understand the Standard Event

The BO I_EnterpriseProjectTP_3 exposes the following standard event relevant to our scenario:

 

interface;
...
define behavior for I_EnterpriseProjectTP_3 alias EnterpriseProject
...
use event EntProjElmntChgd_2;
...

 

The event parameter is defined as:

 

@event.sapObjectNodeTypeKey: [ { element: 'ProjectElementUUID' } ]
define root abstract entity D_EntProjEntProjElmntChgd_2
{
ProjectElementUUID : /s4ppm/tv_entity_guid;
ProjectElement     : /s4ppm/tv_external_id;
ProcessingStatus   : dpr_tv_proc_status_own;
_Before: composition [1..1] of D_EntProjEntProjElmntChgdBfr;
}

 

Key points:
- ProjectElementUUID           — identifies exactly which WBS element was changed
- ProcessingStatus                  — the new status after the change
- _Before-ProcessingStatus  — the status before the change, allowing you to detect a status transition


Step 2: Create the Event Handler Class

First, create a global ABAP class that declares it handles events of I_EnterpriseProjectTP_3. This class acts as the
registration point — the actual logic lives in a local class inside it:

 

  CLASS zcl_enterproj_events_handler DEFINITION
    PUBLIC ABSTRACT FINAL
    FOR EVENTS OF I_EnterpriseProjectTP_3.
  ENDCLASS.

  CLASS zcl_enterproj_events_handler IMPLEMENTATION.
  ENDCLASS.

 


Step 3: Implement the Business Logic

 

 CLASS ltc_event DEFINITION
    INHERITING FROM cl_abap_behavior_event_handler.
    PRIVATE SECTION.
      METHODS handle_elmnt_changed
        FOR ENTITY EVENT entprojelmntchgd_2
        FOR EnterpriseProject~EntProjElmntChgd_2.
  ENDCLASS.

  CLASS ltc_event IMPLEMENTATION.

    METHOD handle_elmnt_changed.

      DATA lt_block TYPE TABLE FOR UPDATE
        I_EnterpriseProjectTP_3\\EntProjectElementBlockFunction.

      " Only process elements whose status just transitioned to Released (10)
      LOOP AT entprojelmntchgd_2 ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_element_chg>).
        IF <fs_element_chg>-ProcessingStatus        = '10'
        AND <fs_element_chg>-_before-ProcessingStatus <> '10'.
          APPEND VALUE #(
            ProjectElementUUID             = <fs_element_chg>-ProjectElementUUID
            EntProjTimeRecgIsBlkd          = abap_false
            EntProjStaffExpensePostgIsBlkd = abap_false
            EntProjServicePostingIsBlkd    = abap_false
            EntProjPurchasingIsBlkd        = abap_false
            EntProjOtherExpensePostgIsBlkd = abap_false
            %control = VALUE #(
              EntProjTimeRecgIsBlkd          = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
              EntProjStaffExpensePostgIsBlkd = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
              EntProjServicePostingIsBlkd    = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
              EntProjPurchasingIsBlkd        = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
              EntProjOtherExpensePostgIsBlkd = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
            )
          ) TO lt_block.
        ENDIF.
      ENDLOOP.

      IF lt_block IS NOT INITIAL.
        MODIFY ENTITIES OF i_enterpriseprojecttp_3 IN LOCAL MODE
          ENTITY EntProjectElementBlockFunction
            UPDATE FIELDS (
              EntProjTimeRecgIsBlkd
              EntProjStaffExpensePostgIsBlkd
              EntProjServicePostingIsBlkd
              EntProjPurchasingIsBlkd
              EntProjOtherExpensePostgIsBlkd
            )
            WITH lt_block
          REPORTED DATA(ls_reported)
          MAPPED DATA(ls_mapped)
          FAILED DATA(ls_failed).
      ENDIF.

    ENDMETHOD.

  ENDCLASS.

 

 

Step4 : Test the Scenario

1. Open the Project Control app in SAP S/4HANA Cloud 

2. Select a project with one or more WBS elements that have Block Functions set

3. Change the Processing Status of one WBS element with block function setted and then released the processing status

4. Verify that the Block Functions on that WBS element have been cleared automatically

Summary

By consuming the standard EntProjElmntChgd_2 event from I_EnterpriseProjectTP_3, you can react to WBS element status
changes and automate downstream business logic — in this case, removing posting restrictions when an element is
released.

This approach is:

- Non-invasive — no modification to standard code
- Reliable — runs in the same LUW, framework handles commit and rollback
- Extensible — the same pattern applies to other events on the same BO

SAP S/4HANA Cloud for enterprise portfolio and project management

entry.title: Error KI100 during Vendor Condition Contract Settlement with Cross Company delivery
entry.content:
Hello,

I have one document with one single item that we sell to our client but procure from a different one - I sell from Germany but deliver out of Poland. My billing document is correctly posted to the partner profit center and we face no problem in the O2C process, but, when I try to run a settlement where this document is to be found I get the KI100 error because of the different CO account assignment - when we debug we see that we have the correct profit center (the partner profit center) in our settlement document to be created but still the system checks the other profit center for whatever reason I can't understand.

 

I don't think the option to have the message defined as warning would be the trick - what impact would it have on other processes? besides this I could not find anything at all that would be Condition Contract Settlement related and as mentioned outside CCS (we are still in ECC) everything works well.

Would someone have crossed this topic already?

 

Thanks in advance,

Paula.

entry.title: SAP Asset Performance Management Goes CAP-Based: Why This Matters
entry.content:
SAP Asset Performance Management (SAP APM) continues to evolve as part of SAP's cloud-first strategy. One of the most significant changes is the transition to Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP)-based applications on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP). While this may appear to be an architectural change, it also introduces new possibilities for user experience, extensibility, integration, and application development.

Why CAP?

CAP is SAP's recommended framework for building cloud-native applications on SAP BTP. By adopting CAP, SAP APM gains a modern and standardized architecture that supports scalability, easier maintenance, and faster innovation while following the clean-core approach.

For customers, this means future enhancements can be delivered more efficiently without impacting standard SAP functionality.

What's New?

Modern SAP Fiori Experience

CAP enables more flexible and responsive SAP Fiori applications. Organizations can design role-based screens that present relevant information to planners, reliability engineers, maintenance supervisors, and technicians, resulting in a simpler and more productive user experience.

Easier Extensibility

Every business has unique maintenance processes. CAP makes it easier to build side-by-side extensions on SAP BTP—such as custom workflows, validations, KPIs, and dashboards—without modifying standard SAP applications. This simplifies future upgrades and keeps custom developments separate from the core solution.

Better Integration

SAP APM increasingly works alongside solutions such as SAP S/4HANA, SAP Integration Suite, SAP Build, SAP Event Mesh, and AI services available on SAP BTP. CAP provides a consistent development model for connecting these services and creating end-to-end asset management processes.

Faster Development

Developers benefit from reusable data models, OData services, built-in security, and standardized development practices. This reduces development effort while improving code quality and long-term maintainability.

Why Should SAP Professionals Care?

For consultants, CAP opens opportunities to deliver business-focused innovations with modern SAP Fiori applications and flexible extensions. For developers, it offers a cloud-native framework aligned with SAP's long-term technology roadmap.

Most importantly, organizations gain a platform that supports innovation while maintaining a clean core—helping reduce upgrade complexity and accelerate digital transformation.

Final Thoughts

The move to CAP-based applications is more than a technical modernization—it represents the future direction of SAP APM. With improved UI flexibility, simplified extensibility, stronger integration capabilities, and cloud-native development, CAP provides a solid foundation for the next generation of intelligent asset management solutions.

As SAP continues investing in Intelligent Asset Management, understanding CAP and SAP BTP will become an essential skill for consultants, architects, and developers who want to build future-ready enterprise maintenance solutions.

References

  • SAP Help Portal – SAP Asset Performance Management
  • SAP Help Portal – Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP)
  • SAP Business Technology Platform Documentation
  • SAP Community Blogs and Learning Resources
  • Relevant SAP Knowledge Base Articles (KBAs)

entry.title: Ask Me Anything with SAP Champions – Monthly Series (Session 6)
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

💬What would you ask someone who's led SAP Finance transformations and global rollouts?

After years working on SAP Finance, international SAP rollouts, and project management, I've realized something: the most valuable insights don't always come from presentations or documentation—they come from honest conversations.

Every implementation brings new challenges. Every SAP rollout teaches unexpected lessons. And every question, no matter how simple or complex, has the potential to help someone else facing the same situation.

That's why I'm excited to participate in Session 6 of my Ask Me Anything (AMA) series! 🎉

This is your opportunity to ask me anything about:
✅SAP Finance (FI/CO)
✅Global SAP rollouts
✅Project & Program Management
✅SAP transformation and organizational change
✅Managing international stakeholders and cross-functional teams
✅Lessons learned from real-life SAP implementations
✅Building a successful career in SAP consulting and leadership

Whether you're preparing for your first rollout, leading a transformation program, tackling a challenging project, or simply curious about life in the SAP world, I'd love to hear your questions.

📌Leave your question in the comments.

🔒Prefer to stay anonymous? You can submit your question here: Ask Me Anything – Daniel Mateo

Do not miss the opportunity and submit it before 10th of August 2026! Throughout the month, I'll select questions to answer in a dedicated video recap, sharing practical advice, real project experiences, and lessons learned from the field. 

Let's learn from each other and keep strengthening the SAP community—one question at a time.

Looking forward to reading your questions! 🚀
